book_claims.claim: Source of folate (46 mcg per ½ cup canned, 12% DV), iron (2 mg per ½ cup canned, 11% DV), and magnesium (35 mg per ½ cup canned, 9% DV).
book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein source providing multiple micronutrients (folate for DNA synthesis, iron for immune function, magnesium for enzymatic reactions) critical for longevity; legumes are a cornerstone of all Blue Zone and centenarian diets.

supplementary_research.finding: Legume consumption (including kidney beans) is the single most consistent dietary predictor of longevity across five Blue Zone centenarian populations; each 20 g/day increase associated with a 7–8% reduction in mortality risk. (PMID 24871476)

Kidney beans contain resistant starch and soluble fiber that act as prebiotics, promoting short-chain fatty acid production (butyrate), reducing gut inflammation, and supporting microbiome diversity linked to healthy aging. (PMID 26853820)

Regular legume consumption reduces fasting blood glucose, LDL cholesterol, and inflammatory markers; kidney beans specifically show favorable glycemic index effects supporting metabolic longevity. (PMID 27723153)

Kidney beans provide complete amino acid profiles when paired with grains, high fiber content, resistant starch, and multiple longevity-relevant micronutrients; their protein quality index is adequate for plant-based longevity diets.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: High in both soluble fiber (cholesterol-lowering) and resistant starch (prebiotic); fermented in colon to produce butyrate
Lower IGF-1 stimulation than animal protein; digestibility enhanced by cooking and soaking
Folate is heat-sensitive; canned beans retain approximately 60–70% of original folate content
Absorption enhanced by vitamin C co-ingestion; inhibited by phytates in same meal

synergies.description: Vitamin C from lemon dramatically enhances non-heme iron absorption from kidney beans — the key strategy for iron adequacy on plant-based longevity diets
Olive oil enhances satiety and micronutrient absorption; the fat-soluble carotenoids present in bean dishes are better absorbed with olive oil
Classic longevity salad pairing in The Longevity Diet; provides complementary textures and nutrients
Kidney beans are a significant plant-based iron source; understanding iron bioavailability from beans is central to plant-forward longevity nutrition
